explicit_text: That morning, I first heard that a plane had hit the World Trade Center, though at first we thought it might have been an accident. Then a second plane hit the other tower, and I realized this was no accident but a terrorist act. This was the start of the September 11 attacks, and it set everything in motion for us that day.

explicit_text: I was in Hancock, Maryland that morning when I got the news about the planes hitting the World Trade Center. I went to the local Ranger station there to meet a fellow Ranger and we started gathering information from our Communications Center. Hancock was the starting point for us before heading toward the National Capital Region.

explicit_text: Our Communications Center serves the entire National Capital Region, including DC and surrounding areas. On September 11, it was the hub for receiving all the incoming information about the attacks, including reports about the Pentagon and other incidents. It was physically located here at Ferry Hill, and we relied on it heavily for coordination.

explicit_text: We learned that a plane had struck the Pentagon, and that was a critical part of the day's events. Traveling from the daycare to the DOT building took us right past the front of the Pentagon, where the damage was visible and emotional for some.
